title: Found the Light of My Life
summary: Your eyes sparkled, and my heart pounded. In a moment that was deeply romantic and beautifully simple, we stood hand in hand and became one — for richer or poorer, in sickness and in health.

title: 新生活
summary: 在记忆深处找回两个人的幸福时光，处处是温馨和欢畅。脸上藏不住的笑，每天一起种花拔草，一起设计添置家当。用不完的时间，洒在小区的散步路上，寂静无事的夜里，卷在书房沙发上一起追剧酣畅。我有一次看完电影痛苦流涕，无法自抑，因为想到将来或许我也会失忆，不能忍受失去的痛苦或直面陌生的现实。还是看看老照片吧，它们让我踏实，提醒我珍惜和你在一起的日子。

title: Our First Child
summary: With the coming of Richard, I also witnessed the metamorphosis of you from a petite lady to a brave mother.  You endured so much pain to give his birth, and never stopped giving all you love and strength to nurture our son. Oh man, I am blessed!
milestones: Meaning of his English name: Richard the King!
Second meaning of his name: Live a rich life
Meaning of his Chinese name: Be smart and unique

title: 三口之家
summary: 游戏已经改变，宇宙中心变得明确。早起晚睡跟着转，一切都很正确。听从老婆的指导，跟随老婆的脚步。从奶爸做起，认认真真努力学习，要做一个合格的爸爸，称职的老公。我自以为已使出洪荒之力，谁看到妈妈日复一日的坚持，幽暗灯光下疲倦的脸庞。亲爱的老婆，我怎么一直这样粗心，没能在意你的青春正在悄然流淌。
milestones: 从此多了一个生日，永记心底

title: On The Move
summary: The world got bigger and we travelled farther. No matter where we went, Mom would always ensure we got 100% prepared. If travelled by car, there would be plenty of food and snack on the trip. If stayed overnight, then the right pieces of clothes and towers (and everything) would fill up the suit cases. You are so organized, my love. Did I tell you - I am spoiled 🙂
milestones: We saw the volcano, and Richard said: 'Trees fell down, burned to death!'

title: 中国行
summary: 每次回国都让人兴奋，然而有去就有还，离别时不仅感叹相聚的短暂。我看到你哭了，回头送别爸妈的泪目中说不尽的留恋和不舍。年纪大了，看一眼少一眼。仔细算来，剩下和父母一起的日子，累计起来不过月余。龙应台说过，养大孩子的过程就是看着他们的背影，渐行渐远。当我们回头一望时，看到的何尝不是背影。回国去看看吧，每年都回家走走吧。爸妈会越走越远，孩子也终将远去，唯念我可以陪着你一起走过这一生。

title: Building Dreams
summary: We traded weekend spontaneity for saving, planning, and building something lasting. It was tedious and beautiful, the quiet architecture of a shared future.

title: Family Adventures
summary: The three of us packed into the car and drove to the shore. Sandcastles, boardwalks, and sticky fingers became the language of our new rhythm.
milestones: Built a sandbox in the yard

title: Loss and Love
summary: We said goodbye to someone we loved too soon. Grief sat with us at the kitchen table, but so did grace, and we learned to hold each other a little tighter.

title: Moving Forward
summary: A new home, a garden, and a mailbox with our name on it. We made a backyard for barbecues, fireflies, and the slow business of growing roots.

title: Milestone Birthday
summary: Forty looked good on us. Surrounded by friends, we laughed about the years behind us and raised a glass to the ones still ahead.

title: New Horizons
summary: Passports stamped, trains caught, and ancient streets wandered. We ate bread in tiny cafés and remembered that the world was still wide open.

title: The Teen Years
summary: School projects, sports games, and the quiet ache of watching someone grow up. We found ourselves in the passenger seat of someone else’s becoming.

title: Side by Side
summary: Career milestones arrived, and we celebrated them together. Victories felt sweeter when shared, and setbacks felt lighter when halved.

title: A Year Apart and Together
summary: The world closed its doors, but our home opened in new ways. Board games, bread baking, and long walks became the scaffolding of resilience.

title: New Puppy
summary: A bundle of chaos with paws arrived. Early mornings, chewed slippers, and unconditional joy reminded us how good it is to welcome new love.

title: Empty Nests and Full Hearts
summary: The house grew quieter, but the silence held new conversations. We rediscovered date nights, lazy mornings, and the version of us that started it all.

title: Vow Renewal
summary: In the backyard, surrounded by those who matter most, we said the words again. They meant more the second time, worn soft by the years.

title: Grand Adventures
summary: We chased sunrises across temples, markets, and unfamiliar cities. Travel reminded us that curiosity is a habit that keeps a marriage young.

title: Slowing Down
summary: We said no to more things and yes to quiet. Books, long walks, and simple meals became the luxury we had earned.

title: Twenty Years
summary: Two decades of choosing each other, day after day. We gather the memories, the hard and the golden, and look ahead with gratitude.
